Non-binding Term Sheet — Proposed all-share combination of Novacircuit S.A. (Luxembourg) and Arcturus Grid SE (Germany).
Issuer: Novacircuit S.A., shares admitted to trading on Euronext Paris (Regulated Market); securities type: ordinary shares (placeholder ISIN: XX0000000000).
Structure: Cross-border merger by absorption under Directive (EU) 2017/1132 as amended; exchange ratio to be determined pursuant to independent expert valuation (no figures provided here).
Status of information: Terms summarized from Novacircuit’s public press releases of 12 Feb 2024 and 28 Apr 2024 and documents filed in the AMF/OAM; any numerical guidance in those releases is now stale and subject to update.
Financing: Use of a sustainability-linked bridge facility previously announced on 05 Mar 2024; no new leverage covenants or price-sensitive metrics are included here; materiality assessment will be issuer-relative and qualitative only for this summary.
Inside information/MAR: The expected SPA signing date and board approval are not yet public; a decision to seek delayed disclosure under Article 17(4) MAR is being evaluated for a target signing at 19:00 CET on 22 Nov 2026, subject to strict confidentiality and no-leak conditions.
Closed periods: PDMR dealing is prohibited during the 30-day MAR closed period prior to the already-posted H1 2026 results date (corporate calendar shows 29 Aug 2026), with routine exemptions per ESMA guidance.
Disclosure controls: No selective disclosure to analysts or investors; all communications to be disseminated via a regulated information service and IR role mailbox only.
GDPR (including Art 9): Data room content is limited to business data; special-category personal data is out of scope; any incidental special-category items must be redacted or processed under Art 9(2) with a documented DPIA and minimisation.
Cross-border transfers: Access to pseudonymised HR and ESG datasets shall occur within the EEA; any remote access from the UK or US requires 2021 SCCs and a Transfer Impact Assessment; processor details available via privacy@novacircuit.eu (role inbox).
Sustainability: The combined group anticipates CSRD reporting from FY2026; SFDR Article 8 alignment for the bridge facility’s use of proceeds is targeted based on publicly disclosed KPIs; Taxonomy references rely on the 2025 non-financial statement already published.
Operational update (third party): GridWare GmbH, a non-affiliate supplier, publicly announced on 03 Jan 2026 a logistics software migration; this vendor notice is not part of the Issuer’s forward-looking statements and is not material.
Conditions precedent: EU Merger Regulation clearance, local FDI screenings where applicable, and works council information/consultation; initiation of consultations was publicly noted on 15 May 2026.
Listing/exchange documentation: Any prospectus or shareholder circular will follow the EU Prospectus Regulation and AMF guidance; expected filing windows reflect Euronext trading calendars and are indicative only.
Confidentiality/clean teams: Only aggregated, anonymised operational metrics to be shared with buy-side clean teams; any identifier-shaped codes (e.g., ID 000-XX-000) are placeholders and not government IDs.
Contacts: All enquiries via IR@novacircuit.eu and dealroom@arcturusgrid.eu; no personal emails or direct lines are provided; general AMF helpline details are publicly available.
This summary is for discussion purposes and does not constitute a binding obligation or an offer, and it may be superseded by publicly filed documents in due course.
